In spirituality, tears in a temple are often seen differently.
It is said that when you stand before the Divine, your soul recognizes a presence that the mind cannot fully understand.

The ego becomes quieter.
The heart becomes softer.
And emotions that have been hidden for months, sometimes years, begin to flow out.
These tears are not always a sign of pain.
They can be a sign of surrender.
Of gratitude.
Of karmic release.
Or simply the feeling of being deeply seen by the Divine.
Perhaps that’s why so many people enter a temple with a heavy heart…
And leave feeling lighter than before. 🙏❤️

Your intuition knows you! ⭐️ 

✨ If you chose 1:
You’ve been acting like you’re okay with everything… but there’s one thing that’s been bothering you more than you’d like to admit. You keep telling yourself you’ll deal with it later. Maybe it’s time to stop avoiding that conversation with yourself.

✨ If you chose 10:
You’re tired of repeating the same cycle. Same lesson, different people. Same hope, different outcome. The good news? You’re finally starting to see what needs to change.

✨ If you chose 19:
You’ve been the strong one for so long that people assume you don’t need support. But lately, you’ve been wishing someone would ask if you’re okay for a change.

✨ If you chose 28:
There’s something you want badly, but you’ve been trying to play it cool. Truth is, you think about it more than you admit. Whether it’s love, a person, or a goal, you haven’t let it go, and maybe you’re not supposed to.

Shanidev ke prabhav (jaise Sade Sati ya Dhaiyya) ko shaant karne ke liye Hanuman Ji ki pooja sabse shaktishaali upay maani jaati hai. 🙏🏻

• Shanivaar ko 7 baar Hanuman Chalisa ka paath karein.
• Mangalvaar aur Shanivaar ko Hanuman Ji ko chameli ka tel aur sindoor chadhayein.
• Shanivaar shaam sarson ke tel ka deepak jalayein.
• “Om Hanumate Namah” mantra ka 11 ya 108 baar jap karein.

☀️ Mulank 1 (Sun)
Light a cotton wick dipped in ghee. Ask your mother or sister to rotate it around your body 7 times anti-clockwise to remove Nazar.
🌙 Mulank 2 (Moon)
Ask your mother or sister to take some table salt in her right hand and rotate it from head to toe 7 times anti-clockwise. Dispose of the salt in a sink or toilet.
♃ Mulank 3 (Jupiter)
Visit a temple and sit quietly for 10 minutes. Absorb the positive energy and allow your mind to settle.
☊ Mulank 4 (Rahu)
Take 3 cloves in your right hand and close your fist. Rotate it above your head 9 times anti-clockwise. Place the cloves on a piece of paper and burn them.
☿ Mulank 5 (Mercury)
Hold fresh green grass in your right hand and rotate it around your head 7 times anti-clockwise. Throw it outside your home afterwards.
♀️ Mulank 6 (Venus)
Apply curd on your body before bathing. After your bath, apply a pleasant fragrance or perfume.
☋ Mulank 7 (Ketu)
Take 5 cloves and a pinch of turmeric in your right hand. Rotate them around your body 7 times and then burn them on a piece of paper outside your home.
♄ Mulank 8 (Saturn)
Take a salt-water bath. Afterwards, apply a small amount of mustard oil on your body.
♂️ Mulank 9 (Mars)
Take one whole red chilli in your right hand and rotate it above your head 9 times anti-clockwise. Burn it outside your home.

Money doesn’t grow by doing kanjoosi!
 In astrology, Jupiter (Guru) is the planet of growth and prosperity. When you hold back money unnecessarily, Jupiter’s energy becomes weak, blocking abundance. Spending on self-growth, knowledge, or even small luxuries activates Jupiter and Venus, bringing you wealth and good fortune.
Also, the 9th and 11th houses in your kundli are linked to luck and gains. When you invest in yourself, you strengthen these houses and open new doors for success. Remember, money flows where there’s movement—don’t let it get stuck!
Now let me tell you a secret 🤫, it’s applicable for all the dobs 😎

Every day is traditionally associated with a different planet.

🌙 Monday — Moon (Chandra)
🔥 Tuesday — Mars (Mangal)
🌿 Wednesday — Mercury (Budh)
✨ Thursday — Jupiter (Guru)
💖 Friday — Venus (Shukra)
🪐 Saturday — Saturn (Shani)
☀️ Sunday — Sun (Surya)

Each planet is believed to carry its own unique energy and qualities. Some are linked to peace, some to courage, some to wisdom, and others to responsibility.

That’s why wearing colors according to the day is not simply about superstition.

Colors may not change everything. But sometimes, a small conscious choice can help you start the day with a little more intention, confidence, and positivity.

💰 Want to create a home that supports financial growth and abundance? According to Vastu, the energy of your space can influence opportunities, stability, and prosperity.

✨ Try these simple Vastu tips:

💧 Place a water element in the North direction — This zone is associated with career growth and the flow of opportunities. A small fountain, water bowl, or water-themed décor can help activate positive energy.

🔐 Keep cash, jewellery, and important financial documents in the South-West — This direction represents stability and security, making it ideal for safeguarding your valuables and strengthening wealth retention.

🌿 Add healthy green plants in the East direction — The East is linked to growth, vitality, and new beginnings. Healthy plants can encourage expansion, fresh opportunities, and financial progress.

🗑️ Avoid placing a dustbin in the North-East — Considered one of the most sacred zones of a home, clutter or waste in this area may disrupt positive energy and hinder prosperity.

Small changes can make a big difference in how your space feels and functions.

In Vedic astrology, Panchak is a recurring lunar period that occurs when the Moon transits through five specific nakshatras. Among its different forms, **Mrityu Panchak** is traditionally considered a time to exercise caution, patience, and mindfulness.

However, it’s important to remember that this period is **not meant to create fear or panic**. Rather, it encourages us to slow down, think carefully before making major decisions, and focus on introspection instead of impulsive action.

Many people use this time for spiritual practices, self-reflection, and planning for the future rather than rushing into new beginnings.

Think of it as a cosmic reminder to pause, reflect, and move forward with greater awareness. 🌙✨

Swipe through to understand its significance and how to navigate this period wisely.

The 6:6 Portal is here, bringing the amplified energy of the number 6 — associated in numerology with love, harmony, beauty, abundance, responsibility, and emotional balance.

When this energy is doubled, it’s considered a powerful time to focus on your heart’s desires and align yourself with what you wish to attract.

What can you manifest during this portal?
• Self-love and inner healing
• Healthy relationships
• Family harmony
• Financial abundance
• Creativity and artistic success
• A beautiful and peaceful home environment

Simple 6:6 Manifestation Ritual:

1️⃣ Find a quiet space and light a white or pink candle.
2️⃣ Write down 6 intentions as if they have already manifested.
Example: “I am surrounded by loving and supportive relationships.”
3️⃣ Read them aloud 6 times with gratitude and conviction.
4️⃣ Visualize yourself already living that reality.
5️⃣ End by saying: “I am open to receiving the highest good for me.”

The key is to focus on feelings of gratitude, love, and abundance rather than lack.

The energy you embody is the energy you attract.

Comment ”666” if you’re ready to welcome the blessings of this powerful portal into your life.
